complaint:
I am a resident of Sehore, Madhya Pradesh, and wish to lodge a formal complaint regarding the unregulated and potentially cruel use of horses in commercial tanga horse cart operations in the city. In response to my RTI application number MPRTI 2504 523528, the Nagar Palika Parishad Sehore has officially stated that no bye laws, registration mechanisms, welfare guidelines, veterinary checks, licensing systems, or designated horse cart routes exist within city limits. This lack of oversight has led to recurrent traffic disruptions and serious welfare concerns. The horses are being used commercially without any provision for rest, water, medical care, or humane treatment, which is a violation of Section 11 of the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act 1960 that prohibits overworking or neglecting working animals. Furthermore, the Performing Animals Registration Rules 2001, particularly Rule 3 and Rule 8, which mandate registration, veterinary supervision, rest periods, and humane transport for animals used publicly, are being ignored. This situation closely resembles the case of Animals and Birds Charitable Trust versus MCGM and Others decided by the Bombay High Court in 2015, where the court banned the operation of horse drawn Victoria carriages in Mumbai due to cruelty and lack of regulation. That judgment emphasized the need for licensing, welfare oversight, and humane treatment. The Supreme Court in the case of Animal Welfare Board of India versus A Nagaraja in 2014 further affirmed that animals have a right to live with dignity under Articles 21 and 51A g of the Constitution.

I respectfully urge the Animal Welfare Board of India to conduct an inspection of tanga operations in Sehore, direct the local authorities to frame and implement proper regulatory mechanisms, mandate the registration and welfare monitoring of working horses, and enforce compliance with applicable laws to protect the wellbeing of both animals and citizens.
place_of_incident: Behind Kuldeep Sethi's Bungalow Radha Niwas Nehru Colony Sehore

complaint: I am writing to lodge a formal complaint regarding a disturbing case of animal cruelty currently unfolding in Kasrawad District Khargone Madhya Pradesh. In response to a recent dog bite incident reported in Khargone city an unverified and likely unauthorized stray dog catching drive has extended its reach into Kasrawad which is approximately 36 kilometers away from the original site of concern. Local residents including myself have witnessed innocent and non-aggressive stray dogs being forcefully captured in an inhumane manner causing visible harm and distress. One particular dog who had peacefully resided in our neighborhood for the past three to four years and was known to be gentle and friendly was seen with his mouth bleeding after being caught using crude and violent methods. This specific dog was familiar to many local families who regularly fed and cared for him and his mistreatment has caused significant emotional distress among the community. It is deeply troubling that these dogs are reportedly being relocated to forest areas a practice that is not only inhumane but also illegal under the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act 1960 and the Animal Birth Control Dogs Rules which mandate sterilization and return to the original location not abandonment. Releasing domesticated strays into the wild is equivalent to a death sentence exposing them to starvation predation and extreme weather and is in direct contradiction to upreme Court rulings including AWBI vs PETA Ors. 2015 which uphold the rights of the rights of stray dogs to live without arbitrary relocation. Moreover there seems to be no veterinary supervision legal documentation or involvement of recognized animal welfare organizations during these operations which raises serious concerns regarding both the legality and morality of these actions. As a concerned citizen and animal lover I request the Animal Welfare Board of India to take urgent cognizance of this issue intervene to stop this illegal and cruel operation in Kasrawad and direct the responsible authorities to ensure humane lawful and scientifically sound methods such as sterilization and vaccination are employed instead. Furthermore I appeal for an investigation into the conduct of the personnel involved in these operations and request that any dogs already relocated be rescued and returned if possible. The voiceless animals of our town deserve protection under the law and I urge your esteemed board to act swiftly to prevent further injustice and suffering.
place_of_incident: Nagar parishad chouk kasrawad

complaint: FORMAL COMPLAINT Request AWBI official action for negligence that resulted in the CRUEL death INJURY and DROWNING of a full-grown tigress, after it fell on 07.05.2025 into an open deep hazardous well without protective parapet wall in the reserves Kumbhpani buffer area. village Dawajhir, Tehsil-Chand, Dt-Chhindwara. I am obligated to file this formal complaint on the cruel and accidental death of a full-grown adult tigress on date 07.05.2025 as a result of falling into an open deep well without parapet border wall.  This revenue farm land  is located near Dawajhir village, Tehsil-Chand in District Chhindwara, opposite the Ghurla-tekri forest in the buffer area of Pench Tiger Reserve Chhindwara.  Village Dawajhir is listed in ESZ notification Dt 06.11.2019 of Pench TR in S.No. 64. Such incidents of wild animals falling into open wells are common in Pench Tiger Reserve M.P areas, and the park administration had taken no real steps till date to prevent such fatalities. The aforementioned adult tigress death is noted as S. No. 73 on the NTCA portal list of Mortality for the year 2025. The Field Director Mr. Devprasad J and Dy Director Mr. Rajneesh Singh of Pench Tiger Reserve M.P ignored the risk, presence of open hazardous wells without parapet wall as a threat to the tigress life and had failed to coordinate with revenue department and neglected to ensure the construction of protective parapet boundary wall around open wells that are located inside the tiger reserve. Thus, this is a case of cruelty to tigress due to Injury and drowning, causing slow death and tigress accidental death caused by the forest department negligence and dereliction of duty. Thus, accidental death of tigress should be investigated and dealt with by the AWBI in accordance with the law and preventive action be conveyed to the Chief Wildlife Warden, Bhopal Madhya Pradesh. The forest beat guard camp is only 500 meters from the well, and the guard reported that he learnt of the tigress death at 6 p.m., although locals claim that the tiger fell into the well early in the morning, when the tiger is most active. Hence, injured with broken nails, tigress suffered long hours inside the deep well in water. The incident occurred in a non remote area, and as per locals the forest department does not help them and ignores during mananimal conflicts. Myself, after witnessing the tigress rescue operation on 07.05.2025 lasting from 06:00 pm until 09:30 pm, tigress died around 10:45 pm. I humbly state that, the AWBI initiate action against the Field Director and Dy Director of Pench Tiger Reserve M.P for the cruel death due to Injury and drowning causing slow death in accordance with the law.
place_of_incident: Near Dawajhir village, (opposite Ghurla-Tekri Forest), Tehsil-Chand, Dt-Chhindwara.

complaint:
Subject: Urgent Complaint Regarding the Relocation and Mishandling of Street Dogs, and Alleged Mismanagement at the Dog Shelter

Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing to file an urgent complaint regarding the relocation of street Dog, which I used to feed and care for on the streets of my locality, and the subsequent mishandling and negligence I have observed at the shelter where my dog was taken for vaccination. I believe there is a serious issue of animal cruelty, mismanagement, and dishonest practices involved, and I request your immediate intervention in this matter.

I had been caring for a street dog in my colony, and when the dog was taken for vaccination on the complaint of vijay thakur in CM helpline, it was taken by an individual named Rahul Jaiswal, who was involved with , Vijay Thakur, in my area. Vijay Thakur has a known history of regularly filing complaints about street dogs through the CM helpline, and it appears that his actions are motivated by personal preferences rather than the well-being of the animals.

They assured to their neighbours who were opposing them  that the dog would be vaccinated and then returned to the street where it had been living. However, when I visited the dog house to check on dog, I discovered several distressing issues.

Upon inspection, I found that the conditions of the dog house were appalling. The hygiene was extremely poor, and many of the dogs there were in very bad physical and emotional condition. I was shocked to discover that the records of the dogs were manipulated , and when I attempted to take photographs or videos to document the situation, I was denied permission and forcibly removed from the premises. complaint: I submit this formal complaint to the Animal Welfare Board of India regarding serious veterinary negligence resulting in the death of my companion dog Brutus and raising broader concerns of unsafe off label drug use inadequate emergency care and lack of accountability in private veterinary practice. The incident occurred at Panacea Veterinary Clinic Sector 53 Noida Uttar Pradesh. I am approaching Delhi based statutory authorities due to proximity functional oversight mechanisms and the need for impartial regulatory review. This complaint involves two interlinked lapses prolonged off label and excessive ivermectin administration and negligent handling of a critical medical emergency without timely referral or stabilization. Brutus was a previously healthy 4.5 year old American Bully weighing approximately 45 kg. Between 5 September and 25 September 2025 he was treated for a mild skin condition at the clinic. During this period he received weekly injections of Ivermectin 9 mg livestock formulation along with Trivivet injections and multiple oral medications. This dosing equates to approximately 0.20 mg per kg every week whereas standard veterinary references recommend ivermectin at such doses only at longer intervals or under close short term supervision. After the fourth dose Brutus developed vomiting and diarrhoea. Blood tests dated 30 September 2025 showed elevated liver enzymes consistent with drug induced hepatic injury. Despite these warning signs the same treatment protocol was continued without dose adjustment enhanced monitoring or referral to a specialist. On 3 October 2025 Brutus presented in a collapsed state with severe respiratory distress inability to stand abdominal distension and absence of urination. Despite the critical condition the case was handled as an outpatient. Intravenous fluids and X rays were performed and I was instructed to travel approximately 20 km to Delhi for an ultrasound resulting in a delay of over four hours. The ultrasound confirmed massive ascites hepatomegaly and an empty bladder indicating advanced organ compromise. Even at this stage no referral was made to a facility with oxygen intensive care transfusion or surgical capability. I was instead advised to return after several hours reportedly due to clinic closing time. Later that evening following attempts at abdominal needle drainage without oxygen stabilization Brutus passed away. This sequence reflects a failure to recognize and manage a veterinary emergency and a breach of basic emergency care and referral protocols. Following Brutus death the clinic has not provided a written treatment summary or diagnosis for 3 October 2025 clear readable prescriptions or drug administration records or any explanation of clinical decision making during the emergency. Only an informal condolence message was received without medical details or accountability. Key issues for regulatory review include repeated off label ivermectin use at unsafe intervals without informed consent or monitoring failure to stabilize or refer a critically ill animal to an equipped facility possible use of inadequately qualified staff for injections and absence of proper documentation transparency and post incident disclosure. I respectfully request that the competent veterinary and animal welfare authorities conduct an independent inquiry into the handling of Brutus case examine potential violations of veterinary professional conduct regulations direct the clinic to furnish complete treatment and drug administration records and initiate disciplinary and legal action as warranted under applicable animal welfare and criminal laws. This complaint is submitted not only for redress of my personal loss but to help prevent similar avoidable deaths that often go unreported due to lack of awareness and regulatory follow through. Basit Nisar Left clinic, soon the DM inquiry initiated, so his creditial as valid practicing vet could not be validated in clinic even he was associated there 3 years 8 months.
place_of_incident: Panacea Veterinary Clinic, Main Rd, opp. Manas Hospital, Block E, Sector 53, Noida, Uttar Pradesh 20

Despite repeated complaints and alerts regarding the inhumane conditions of langoors, the Forest Department only became active after PETAs involvement. Even then, only 4 langoors were rescued, while others are still being tied at multiple locations by the organisers.

The langoors are being kept:

 In direct sunlight, with no proper food or water, appearing dehydrated and stressed.
 Exposed to loud music, laser lights, drones, crackers, and thousands of people, causing severe trauma.
Forced into unnatural behavior, instead of being handled using scientifically approved wildlife care methods.

Authorities/organisers have justified this by saying if langoors are not brought, desi monkeys will destroy diyas, which is no excuse for subjecting animals to cruelty.

Urgent actions requested:

1. Conduct a full inquiry into who is responsible for bringing, tying, and using langoors repeatedly.
2. Direct the Forest Department and UP Wildlife authorities to Issue a strict ban on the use of desi langoors or any wildlife for public events in the future.
3. Release a press statement about the inhumane conditions observed, and warn organisers and the public.

The repeated mistreatment and lack of proactive action by authorities highlight gross negligence and inhumanity. Immediate intervention is critical to protect these voiceless animals and prevent future cruelty.

Attachment: Video evidence of langoors tied at multiple locations at the CM event site, Ayodhya.

Yours sincerely,
Pragya Gupta
Animal Rights Activist / Ayodhya Animal Welfare
9056583401
Ayodhya, Uttar Pradesh
place_of_incident: Ayodhya

Heart Breaking Incident The dog was left unattended in a locked car with windows rolled fully up in mid day heat, its tongue was protruding, chest heaving, then lifeless. Bystanders observed it gasp for 90 minutes before attempts to rescue it republicworld.com. Locals tried to warn the owners, and someone dutifully called a mechanic to unlock the vehicle: but all intervention arrived too late, and the family returned only to find their pet already gone . 2. Legal Violations : A Grim Portrait of Criminal Cruelty Under the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960: Sec. 11 1h and i : Locking an animal in a manner causing suffering. Sec. 11 1A : Abandonment. Sec. 27 1 : Transportation causing avoidable suffering. Sec. 12 : Cruelty by reckless negligence. This isnt oversight : it is a reckless, inexcusable breach of the duty we owe to sentient beings in our care. 3. Supporting Evidence Witness Testimonies: Multiple onlookers confirm that beggars of mercy were ignored when they advised the owners against locking the dog in the car. Viral Video: Visual proof of the dogs distress, clearly showing its last breaths gasping within the sealed cabin hindustantimes.com, news9live.com, lokmattimes.com. Immediate seizure of the centrally locked vehicle and investigation into the owners failure to act. 2. Prompt statements from eyewitnesses under Section 161 CrPC : especially those who pleaded for mercy. 3. Community safety mandate for ride hailing and parking operators in Vrindavan, Mathura : no pet should ever be locked unattended again. 7. complaint:
To
The Secretary,
Animal Welfare Board of India
Government of India.

Subject  Illegal display of fishes through Underwater Tunnel at Shahjahanpur Exhibition Fair O.C.F. Ground Shahjahanpur Uttar Pradesh Violation of Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act. And Camel ride is also going on

Respected Sir,
I humbly request that I, Shrish Dixit, Honorary Animal Welfare Representative , Shahjahanpur, would like to draw your attention to a very important matter.
In an exhibition fair going on at Shahjahanpur District O.C.F. Ground, a large number of live fishes are being displayed through Underwater Tunnel for the attraction of the general public. Using alligator gar fish etc exotic fishes
This display is completely illegal, unethical and in violation of the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960 and the guidelines laid down by AWBI for the following reasons

Main objections
Any public display of living creatures without prior permission of AWBI is illegal.

1. Please inform whether prior permission has been obtained from your Board for this Underwater Tunnel,  If yes, please share the certified copy of that permission letter and the copy of the related fee payment receipt.
2. Due to the excessive crowd and heat in day daytime, the fishes are looking very agitated and this is making their condition very pathetic and there is also a danger to their lives.
3. It appears that there is no proper arrangement of adequate oxygen, temperature control, and other biological requirements for the fishes. In such a situation, the lives of these living creatures are in danger, which is a clear violation of Section 11 of the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act.
4. This seems to be an experiment done not for educational purposes, but only for entertainment and commercial gain.
5. This type of unscientific and unauthorized demonstration sends a wrong message about animal rights to the public, especially children, which is against the objectives of AWBI.

Earlier also, similar  Fish Tunnel demonstrations have been stopped by AWBI at many places, hence immediate action is required in this case as well.

Request
You are humbly requested to take immediate cognizance of this matter and

1. If permission has been taken, then its documents should be shared. If permission has not been taken, then this illegal activity should be stopped immediately.
2. Legal action should be ensured against the concerned organizers and responsible persons.
3. Instructions should be issued to the local administration to stop such activities in future.

I expect your immediate and fair action in this matter so that the basic principles of animal welfare can be protected in our country.

Enclosed

1. Photographs, Video of the event

Thank you
Shrish Dixit
Honorary Animal Welfare Representative, Shahjahanpur
Mobile number- 7081977397
Email- dixitshrishspn@gmail.com
place_of_incident: OCF, Ordinance clothing factory ground shahjahanpur Uttar Pradesh

Even today, I send milk and bread for these dogs every day and one of my friend go to feed them regularly, but the colony residents unnecessarily object that lady from feeding and disturb the dogs.

The residents of the colony unite together, identified these dogs and beat them with sticks, and they also try to chase away the lady who goes to feed them. With Regards

complaint:
I am writing this to bring to your notice the Inhumane and Illegal treatment to stray dogs in our society. Certain individuals and society Maintainance team are forcibly removing stray dogs from their natural habitat in a cruel manner. Tying their feet, beating them with sticks, and relocating them to unfamiliar areas far from their home. Such acts are not only barbaric but in Violation of AWBI guidelines and Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960. Stray dogs are protected under the law, and their forced relocation without proper rehabilitation measures is illegal.
This cruel practice not only causes immense suffering to the animals but also disrupts the ecological balance. Stray dogs are territorial creatures, and forcibly displacing them leads to distress, starvation, and even death. Moreover, this method does not provide a long-term solution to managing the stray dog population. Instead, humane alternatives like sterilization and vaccination programs, as recommended by AWBI, should be implemented.
I urge you to take immediate action to investigate and put an end to this brutality. Those responsible should be held accountable, and strict measures should be put in place to ensure the humane treatment of stray animals. Additionally, efforts should be made to spread awareness about ethical and lawful methods of managing stray dogs.

I request a prompt response outlining the steps being taken to address this issue. Looking forward to your swift action to protect these innocent animals.

I am a community stray feeder in my residential society and facing tremendous harassment from fellow residents for long time. Residents are posting continuous messages in society whatsapp groups instructing feeders to stop feeding strays inside society. Despite educating the residents about rules and guidelines, residents are continuously posting provocative and instigating statements in whatsapp groups, accusing me for rising dog population, for dog violence, etc. I would like to bring to your kind attention that there is already a number of incidents recorded in Delhi NCR about physical lynching of stray feeders. I am living under constant fear of being physically assaulted. 

Various guidelines clearly say that community dogs are a part of the society and cannot be driven out. The AOA or RWA is not allowed to frame any guidelines of their own regarding community dogs

Whereas, I, along with other feeders, have taken positive initiatives as follows,
1.	Sterilization Continuation of sterilization drive of community dogs as mandated by authorities to help prevent increase in numbers. This is already being done and majority are already sterilized
2.	Vaccination Vaccination of community dogs to keep them healthy and prevent any grave danger to residents. A handful of community dog feeders have already initiated this and majority of the community dogs are already vaccinated
3.	Adoption Adoption drive for community dogs through which the overall number of community dogs in the society has already been reduced In driving all these initiatives, sufficient costs have been incurred from our pocket.

People are normally scared of dogs which is creating the biggest hurdle. There are residents who despite being thoroughly educated and literate feign absolute ignorance of animal laws and there is always a clamor for dislocating strays from the society. In addition to this, there are some nasty residents who want to capitalize this matter for making political gains within the society - elections for coming to power in the Panchsheel Hynish Apartment Owners Association. Even though there has not been any incident of dog attack or dog bites, some residents are always spreading fear that the number of dog attacks and dog bites is increasing day by day. Any isolated incident of scooty seat cover taring by dogs is made a big hue and cry. During extreme weather, like heat, cold or rain, stray dogs suffer very heavily and they tend to get inside the residential towers. To prevent this, we made attempts of creating comfortable temporary shelters inside society premises where dogs can remain comfortably. But residents have removed those shelters leaving the dogs exposed to extreme weather. And simultaneously blaming feeders for entry of dogs into towers. 

I have been constantly engaged with the Residential association of our society to identify feeding points and feeding timing inside our society. But due to constant opposition from residents, reaching a favorable conclusion is far from sight. 

Earlier I use to feed twice a day. With increasing opposition of residents, I am compelled to do it once at late night when citizen movement is negligible. Keeping a stray hungry for day long time increase the risks of dog menace more. Moreover, because of feeding the strays at very latenight hours, it is disturbing my biological cycle and daily routine very badly. 

I am now a subject of insult, humiliation, derogatory remarks, constant intimidation and constant harassment by fellow residents. The environment in my society is very hostile and negative against me and is posing a serious risk to my safety and security. People are ganging up in whatsapp groups against me and are talking against my feeding activities in a campaign mode. My attempts of lodging a police complaint have also gone into vain and police have clearly said they cannot take action against such acts. Media also does lot of exaggerated reporting and misreporting about dog violence related matters. The constant hostile environment being carefully crafted by residents against me is causing deterioration in my health conditions and I am now a patient of depression and mental issues. 

I am attaching some screenshots of one resident posting hostile comments about feeding dogs in our society. He is clearly saying to stop feeding, he is clearly asking to keep dogs hungry, which is nothing but animal cruelty. This is happening in the same group where I am part of. People know very well that I am a stray feeder. Hence, there is constant comments against stray feeding, with fingers pointing at me, and residents also supporting such illegal comments. 

I am not getting any support from police. Taking legal action through the court is costly and lengthy. Daily life is a mental stress in such hostile circumstances. I am requesting you with folded hands to intervene and take stringent actions against such residents who are making my life difficult.

complaint: Subject: Complaint Against Illegal Actions by Apartment Owners Association AOA Secretary Regarding Community Dogs Respected Sir/Madam I am writing to bring to your urgent attention a matter of serious concern regarding the illegal actions being promoted by the Secretary of our Apartment Owners Association AOA. The Secretary has been encouraging residents to collectively expel community dogs from the society premises, which is not only unethical but also a clear violation of established laws and guidelines. Details of the Incident: The Secretary of our AOA has recently called upon the residents to come together and forcefully remove community dogs from the society. This act is illegal as per the Animal Welfare Board of India guidelines and the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960. Furthermore, the Secretary has urged residents to take photographs of those who care for or feed these dogs, fostering an atmosphere of hostility and resentment within the community. Relevant Legal Provisions: Article 51A of the Indian Constitution mandates compassion towards all living creatures, including community dogs. Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960: It is illegal to cause harm or cruelty to animals, including community dogs. Forcefully removing them from their established territory or subjecting them to any form of harassment falls under this category. Animal Birth Control Rules, 2001 as mandated by the Supreme Court of India prohibits the relocation of community dogs from their territory. They must be sterilized, vaccinated, and returned to the same area after the procedure. Impact: This attempt to incite residents against the presence of community dogs not only violates the laws and guidelines but also subjects these animals to unnecessary distress and potential harm. The lack of opposition from the rest of the AOA members further exacerbates the situation, creating an unsafe and hostile environment for both the community dogs and the people who care for them. Request for Action: I respectfully request the AWBI to intervene in this matter urgently. We need immediate action to prevent the AOA from proceeding with these illegal activities. I also request that the AWBI issue a formal notice to the AOA, reminding them of the legal implications of their actions and the importance of adhering to the laws and guidelines established to protect the welfare of community dogs. I am prepared to provide further details and evidence as required and am hoping for your swift intervention to ensure that the rights of community dogs and those who care for them are safeguarded. Thank you for your attention to this matter. Yours sincerely, Alpa Dedhia

complaint:
Respected Sir/Madam,
I am lodging this grievance with deep anguish regarding a fraudulent and unethical sale by Mr. Vidhya Ratan, a dog seller based in Meerut, who sold us a puppy in clear violation of the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960. Our Key Issues are:
1.The puppy was sold at less than 2 months of age, violating Rule 10.4 which prohibits sale of animals below 8 weeks,
2.The puppy was suffering from a chronic, incurable kidney condition, which the seller concealed at the time of sale, violating Rule 10.6 prohibiting sale of unfit animals and Rule 12 mandatory health certification.
3.Despite repeated treatment, consultations with renowned vets, dialysis also, the puppy passed away in 9 months, leaving us devastated emotionally and financially.
4.The seller was ruthless and unapologetic, offering a replacement puppy at partial cost and asking us to pay transportation charges a completely unacceptable response given the trauma and fraud involved.
5.The entire episode caused mental harassment, and my husband Mr. Gourav Behera has undergone medication for depression triggered by the loss and betrayal.
The actions of Mr. Vidhya Ratan demonstrate deliberate fraud, cruelty, and gross negligence, and we no longer have any trust in dealing with this individual and hence want justice. Documents attached are Vet reports and medical history, WhatsApp communication with seller by me and my husband along with last voice chat, Payment proof and transaction details and photos. Our Request is Immediate investigation and action against the seller under the PCA Act and Pet Shop Rules, 2018, Permanent cancellation of any pet dealing license if any, held by Mr. Vidhya Ratan, Compensation for financial losses and emotional trauma, Ensure that no such illegal sales continue under his name or business and That all proceedings/inquiries be conducted from Odisha where we reside.
Thank you
Akankshya Kar
w/o Gourav Behera
Pet owners

complaint:
Respected Sir/Madam, I am a resident of RITS Villa, Botanda, Sundarpada, Bhubaneswar, and I am writing to bring to your kind attention a matter concerning the well-being of vaccinated stray dogs- 03 adults and 03 kids residing in our locality. I am also in process of facilitating the sterilization process of the adult ones looking into the confirmation from any Government Veterinary Hospital. As part of a compassionate and law-abiding citizen initiative, our family has been feeding these 06 stray dogs living in and around our premises on a regular basis. Besides, the dogs have been vaccinated and I ensure the continuation of the vaccination periodically as per guidelines - The vaccination proff is attached. I hope this is in compliance with the Animal Birth Control-Dogs Rules, 2001, the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960, and several Supreme Court and High Court orders upholding the rights of stray animals to live in their natural territories. Unfortunately, at present a few occupants of our society have raised objections to our efforts and highlighted the issue through WhatsApp group messaging i.e. in the Society Group and are putting their views for the removal or relocation of these dogs and to report to municipal authorities etc by presenting this case to the local Sarapanch - The copy attached. However, As an Indian citizen, what I understand, this goes against the law, as the dogs have already been vaccinated, and as per the guidelines, must be allowed to remain in their original territory without any relocation. For your kind reference, we have been living in the society for more than 1.5 years and all these dogs have taken birth in the premises and from their childhood days our family is taking care of them. But unfortunately, no such complain from any society occupant has been received in this regard earlier, whereas this baseless objection is very recent. In light of this, we respectfully request the following: 1. Guidance and intervention from the AWBI to educate and inform the opposing residents about the legal provisions protecting stray dogs. 2. A formal letter or advisory from the AWBI that we can present to our RWA/society authorities, reinforcing our right to care for these dogs. 3. Assistance in ensuring a peaceful coexistence between human and animal residents of our society. The speechless animals have all rights to survive being taken care by human beings. I seek your support in upholding compassion, legality, and the fundamental duties enshrined under Article 51A-g of our Constitution. Let the educated human beings be educated properly to take care of the speechless ones. 
Looking forward to your kind support and guidance at the earliest. 
All video clips- CCTV are also available in terms of representation as an evidence of vaccination.
Thanking you.

complaint:
I am submitting this complaint to report acts of cruelty to community dogs at ICAR IISWC Research Centre Vasad Gujarat. In November twenty twenty three a stray mother dog gave birth to ten puppies in the campus. Five puppies died due to parvo virus while the family cared for the remaining five. From July twenty twenty four onward repeated office letters and verbal threats were given by officials including Shri M J Kaledhonkar Shri Anand Kumar and Smt Anjali Negi asking us to remove the dogs with threats of eviction and penalty. On twenty eight January twenty twenty five one such letter was issued after which a lactating female vanished and six dependent puppies died of hunger. Later another dog was relocated and died outside its territory. On seventeen June twenty twenty five local pig catchers were called without approval of municipal or veterinary authorities. A pregnant female about fifteen days and an elderly dog with a heart condition were tied and dragged upside down into a rickshaw GJ twenty three AV one six eight seven and abandoned in unknown locations. This was done without veterinary supervision without AWBI recognised animal welfare organisation and without following any lawful procedure. When videos of this incident were shared online on eighteen and nineteen June twenty twenty five we were threatened with defamation and forced to remove the posts. At that time I could not complain officially due to continuous harassment threats of eviction fear of retaliation and also due to the ill health of my father who was already under pressure as an employee of the institute but now I cannot remain silent.

These actions are unlawful under the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act nineteen sixty which prohibits abandonment and unnecessary pain under the Animal Birth Control Dogs Rules twenty twenty three which prohibit relocation and require handling only by AWBI recognised organisations with veterinary oversight and under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita twenty twenty three which penalises killing or maiming of animals and abetment of such acts. They are also against the CCS Conduct Rules which require government servants to maintain integrity and act in a humane lawful manner. I have already sent detailed emails with evidence to support at awbi dot gov dot in on five August thirteen August four September and eight September twenty twenty five from my email animalwelfare one seven six two five at gmail dot com. I also have office letters issued between July twenty twenty four and June twenty twenty five which show threats of eviction and penalties without following due process of law. I request AWBI to urgently take cognizance of this matter to protect my identity at every stage and to inform me if any committee has been launched or if any committee visits the place so that I can also be present and keep my side clear of all doubts. I once again request strict action against the responsible officials so that no other animals or families suffer such unlawful cruelty.

Sincerely
Dharmraj K
place_of_incident: ICAR-IISWC, RESEARCH CENTRE,VASAD OPPOSITE VASAD RAILWAY STATION

I purchased a Shih Tzu Puppy DOB 12-06-2025 from Oscars pet World, Mumbai on 25-07-2025, which was delivered to me in Ahmedabad through car on 26-07-2025 at only 44 days old. The puppy was underage , severely anemic, tick-infested and unfit for sale. Despite veterinary treatment, the puppy died on 08-08-2025 at only 57 days old, just 13 days after arrival. The veterinary reports and death certificate confirm severe anemia, low platelets, hypoproteinemia, hypoglycemia, and tick infestation, all pointing to pre-existing illness. This constitutes animal cruelty, illegal sale of underage puppies, and unethical breeding practices. The seller has refused responsibility and told me to go to Police. I request AWBI to investigate this case, take strict action against the breeder/seller, and prevent such cruelty and malpractice in future. This puppy, whom we named Syros, quickly became a beloved family member. Losing him at just 57 days old has caused us deep emotional pain and distress, apart from financial loss. No family should go through such suffering because of irresponsible and unethical breeding practices. We request AWBIs urgent intervention so that justice is done for Syros and such cruelty is prevented in future.

complaint:
On November 19 2025 a coordinated mass killing of stray dogs occurred in Dondapudi village Gopalapuram Mandal West Godavari district Andhra Pradesh This incident documented by animal welfare activists and corroborated by digital evidence represents not an isolated act of cruelty but a continuation of a systemic state sanctioned pattern of extermination that has plagued the region for years  

The incident occurred exactly 12 days after the Supreme Court of India issued a directive November 7 2025 mandating the removal of stray dogs from institutional areas and just 48 hours after the Andhra Pradesh state government announced a special drive to address the stray dog menace November 17 2025 The temporal proximity of these events suggests that local administrative bodies specifically the Village Panchayat interpreted judicial and state directives for management as tacit authorization for elimination Following the Supreme Court order on 7th November 2025 Principal Secretary S Suresh Kumar directed municipal commissioners to intensify sterilisation and vaccination efforts Crucially the directive filtered down to the district and panchayat levels as a demand for immediate results The Dondapudi massacre occurred just two days after this state wide drive was publicly launched  

THE DONDAPUDI INCIDENT NOVEMBER 19 2025  

31 The Event On the morning of November 19 2025 at approximately 943 AM the Dondapudi Gram Panchayat allegedly executed a mass killing operation Unlike the lawful capture methods described in the states press releases this operation did not involve nets transport trucks or veterinarians  

Based on the pattern established in the region detailed in Section 4 the operation likely involved  

The Hiring of Professional Killers Local gangs often referred to colloquially as dog catchers but functioning as exterminators are typically hired for a flat fee historically 100 to 150 per dog  

The Method The use of crude poisons often cyanide or pesticide injections or poisoned bait meat laced with insecticides  

The Disposal The immediate burial of carcasses in designated dumping yards or remote puramboke lands to hide the evidence before activists or higher authorities could intervene  

32 The Perpetrator The Dondapudi Panchayat Secretary is the primary authority figure responsible for village level sanitation and animal management Official government records identify the Panchayat Secretary Grade IV Digital Assistant for Dondapudi Secretariat Code 10590047 as Pillamgoru Madhu Babu Yadav  

The name Pillamgoru Madhu Babu Yadav comes from the official Andhra Pradesh village ward secretariat Sachivalayam staff listings for Dondapudi Gopalapuram Mandal West Godavari In those records the Digital Assistant Panchayat Secretary post for Dondapudi secretariat code 10590047 or 10590047WEA is shown against the name Pillamgoru Madhu Babu Yadav which is why that name was used when describing the office holder responsible at the Panchayat level  

4 THE PATTERN OF ORGANIZED CRIME A HISTORICAL ANALYSIS The Dondapudi incident is not a deviation from the norm it is a strict adherence to a macabre West Godavari Model of stray dog management By analyzing previous incidents we can establish that mass killing is an institutionalized practice in this district characterized by a specific set of behaviors Secretarial Authorization Poisoning and Clandestine Burial  

Case Study 1 The Lingapalem Massacre July 2021 Location Lingapalem Village West Godavari District same district as Dondapudi Date July 29 to 30 2021 Casualties approximately 300 dogs  

The Modus Operandi In July 2021 residents near the Perantam tank in Lingapalem noticed a foul stench Excavation by activists revealed a mass grave containing nearly 300 decomposing dog carcasses Investigations revealed that the dogs had been killed using poisoned injections  

The Administrative Link Crucially the police investigation FIR registered at Dharmajigudem Police Station named the Panchayat Secretary M Sugunraj and the Village Sarpanch as the prime accused  

Relevance to Dondapudi The Lingapalem case established the legal precedent that Panchayat Secretaries are criminally liable for these acts Yet four years later the Dondapudi Secretary allegedly replicated this exact model This suggests that the administrative consequences suspension or fines are viewed as manageable risks compared to the pressure to remove dogs

complaint:
To

The Concerned Authority

Subject: Seeking Justice for the Killing of Our Cow and Calf

Respected Sir/Madam,

I am writing to bring to your attention a distressing incident involving the killing of our cow and calf, which occurred on June 15, 2023. This incident is linked to an ongoing land dispute, which has been the subject of a court case since March 2022.

The background of the case is as follows:

- In March 2022, my father filed a suit in court regarding a land dispute.
- The court issued a status quo order to both parties in the same month.
- However, the opposing party, Bora Narayana and family, violated the court order and constructed a house on the disputed land in June 2023.
- When we attempted to stop the construction, a disturbing incident occurred, resulting in the alleged poisoning of our cow and calf by Bora Narayana and family, with the support of Village elders Batchu Dhanunjaya Rao, Abothu Gurunadham, Seerapu Murali, Rampatni Chandra Sekharam, and Seerapu Bhairi.

The incident was reported to the Sub-Inspector of Police, Kaviti, and Crime No. 57/2023 was registered under Section 429 IPC. Unfortunately, despite the documentation of the incident, no further action has been taken to date.

Furthermore, our family has been subjected to harassment, communal boycott, and vandalism, including the burning of dry grass and paddy, over the past three years. We have approached the district-level administration and the Superintendent of Police, Shri K.V. Maheswar Reddy, but the case was forwarded to the Mandal-level administration, which has failed to take adequate action.

As a family, we have been animal lovers and have kept cows and other animals for over 100 years. The killing of our cow and calf has caused immense emotional distress, particularly to my parents, who are living in the village.

In view of the above, we respectfully request that you take immediate action to:

- Investigate the killing of our cow and calf and take appropriate action against Bora Narayana and family, and the supporting Village elders.
- Provide protection and support to our family, particularly my parents, who are living in the village.
We seek justice and restoration of our rights, and we hope that you will take our request seriously and take prompt action.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

U.JAGGA RAO

complaint:
Subject: Urgent Request for Action on Animal Cruelty Case in Nandyal Mandal

Respected Sir/Medam

I am Shaik Akbar Basha, an animal welfare activist and a member of the Animal Birth Control Committee in Nandyal Municipality. I had filed a complaint regarding the killing of several street dogs in Nandyal Mandal, Andhra Pradesh. The complaint was submitted via email on 08/02/2024 to the Animal Welfare Board of India, and I received responses from AWBI and the Director of Animal Husbandry, Andhra Pradesh, advising immediate action on the matter.

However, despite these responses, I have not received any updates or actions from the Nandyal District Animal Husbandry Officer. I have made several attempts to follow up, but have not received any replies to my emails, WhatsApp messages, or in-person inquiries at the DAHO office.

The incident happened nearly a year ago, and despite repeated efforts, I have not been provided with any official updates, reports, or actions taken in this case. This lack of response is deeply concerning, especially considering the importance of enforcing animal protection laws.

I respectfully request your intervention in this matter to ensure that immediate action is taken, and the responsible authorities are held accountable. I trust that necessary steps will be taken to ensure justice for these animals and to prevent such cruelty in the future.

complaint: complaint alleges negligence and suspicious circumstances surrounding the death of 19.5-years old captive elephant at the Sri Sadguru chitti baba Ashram ,Rapaka Village of seethanagaram mandal, East Godavari District, Andhra Pradesh.  The elephant suffered from inadequate living conditions, including a concrete flooring causing foot and joint problems and lack of bathing pool crucial for its physical and mental health.  Daily public parades of the elephant led to  unregulated feeding by devotees, risking its health through imbalanced diets ,digestive problems and potential  poisoning, raising concerns about exploitation. inconsistences surround the death of captive elephant  photos lack visible tusks ,and information from caretakers and general physician  Dr.Phaneendra   regarding the cause of death and living conditions is questionable. A public health physician reported inadequate care of  a deceased captive elephant, prompting demands for a thorough independent investigation into the circumstances of its death. A necropsy , expert consultation , and an enclosure inspection were conducted to investigate the elephant death and assess welfare standards. investigators examined feeding records, interviewed witnesses, analyzed food samples and reviewed care takers actions, including handling and adherence to animal welfare protocols. accountability for  negligence or wrong doing regarding the elephant death is necessary, requiring transparent investigation to prevent future incidents.
place_of_incident:
Sri Sadguru chitti Baba Ashram,
Rapaka Village, Seethanagaram madnal, East Godavari District

complaint:
I hope this letter finds you in good health. I am writing to bring to your attention a matter of pressing concern related to the welfare of stray dogs and the harassment faced by individuals, including myself, who have taken it upon themselves to ensure the well-being of these voiceless creatures.

As per the directives of the Honourable Supreme Court of India, it is our fundamental duty to Protect and improve the natural environment, including forests, lakes, rivers, and wildlife, and to have compassion for living creatures. This duty is enshrined in Article 51 A g of the Constitution of India. It is a moral obligation that we uphold and respect. In recent times, I have faced relentless harassment and restraint from some residents of our locality who disagree with my commitment to feed and provide essential care to stray dogs. I firmly believe that if people are abstained from feeding these animals, it will eventually lead to their suffering and even starvation. It is disheartening to witness these voiceless creatures rummaging through drainages and leftovers in search of sustenance. In this context, I kindly request your intervention to direct or issue an order to the municipality and my neighbours to cease harassing those of us who are dedicated to the welfare of these stray dogs. The Honourable Supreme Court has unequivocally upheld the rights of individuals to feed dogs at their residences, and any attempt to restrict or obstruct this compassionate act is in clear violation of the law.  I would like to emphasise that ignoring the Supreme Court order allowing individuals to feed dogs amounts to civil contempt of court. Civil or criminal contempt of court is punishable by imprisonment for up to six months and/or a fine of up to Rupees 2,000. It is crucial that we ensure the enforcement of these directives and laws.
Furthermore, I would like to bring to your attention that my neighbours have been consistently and cruelly beating dogs with stones and sticks when they come to our home for food. This brutal treatment of these innocent animals is not only inhumane but also a blatant violation of the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960.
In light of the above, I kindly request your immediate attention and intervention to protect the welfare of stray dogs and to ensure that individuals who are committed to their well being are not harassed, obstructed, or subjected to acts of cruelty. The principles of compassion, justice, and the laws of our great nation must be upheld. Furthermore, I would like to bring to your attention the recent statements of Prime Minister Narendra Modi in his monthly radio program, Mann Ki Baat. He praised individuals who take care of animals and highlighted inspiring stories, such as the case of Veera, a disabled dog in Coimbatore who received a custom made wheelchair from a compassionate animal lover. The Prime Minister words underscore the importance of compassion and kindness towards all life forms. In addition, the Prime Minister encouraged people to adopt indigenous dogs, a move that is expected to promote the adoption and welfare of local breeds. This demonstrates the government commitment to animal welfare and the importance of caring for our furry friends. In light of the above, I kindly request your immediate attention and intervention to protect the welfare of stray dogs and to ensure that individuals who are committed to their well being are not harassed or obstructed. The principles of compassion, justice, and the laws of our great nation must be upheld.

I look forward to your prompt action in addressing this issue.
place_of_incident: Sheshappa Thota of Andhra Pradesh

complaint: I have already given a complaint on 04.10.2025 to the AWBI to take action on the illegal cattle transportation that is happening in Walayar area of Palakkad District. The AWBI forwarded my complaint to the Kerala Animal Welfare Board, so far no action has been taken on my complaint by the Kerala Board. The police and the officials are supporting this and i doubt it is due to this reason no action has been taken. Hence I request you to take action on this immediately.

I am writing to bring to your urgent attention the ongoing and inhumane transportation of cattle through the Walayar border in Palakkad District, Kerala, primarily destined for the state of Kerala. These acts involve gross violations of the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960, and various transport and welfare regulations for animals.
It has come to my notice that cattle are being illegally transported in extremely cruel and unhygienic conditions. The perpetrators use closed container vehicles to prevent public visibility and scrutiny. The animals are tightly packed into these vehicles without adequate space to stand, breathe, or rest. Many are tied by their legs, and in several disturbing instances, chilli powder is rubbed into their eyes to force them into submission. These acts constitute clear cruelty and cause severe physical and mental distress to the animals.
Moreover, these transporters operate without mandatory documentation, including: Veterinary Health Certificates, Fitness Certificates for transportation, Valid licenses for cattle transport. Shockingly, the local law enforcement and regulatory authorities, including the police at the Walayar check post and the Animal Husbandry Department, have not taken any consistent action to curb these practices. Their inaction has emboldened the violators, and despite multiple cases being registered in the past year, particularly in areas such as Walayar and Wadakkanchery, no meaningful follow-up or penal action has been observed.
As a concerned citizen, I have documented evidence, including details of the vehicles, identities of the persons involved, and photographs clearly depicting the cruelty inflicted upon the animals. These can be produced upon request or submitted as annexures for investigation.
1.	TN-41-AU-8821- Ashok Leyland

In light of the above, I humbly request the Animal Welfare Board of India to:
1.	Take immediate cognizance of this matter and initiate necessary proceedings.
2.	Direct the Director General of Police, Kerala, the Superintendent of Police, Palakkad, and the Joint Regional Transport Commissioner, Palakkad to take strict and time-bound action against the violators.
3.	Ensure that border checks at Walayar are strengthened, and regular inspections by the Animal Husbandry Department are mandated.
4.	Ensure that all transport of animals complies strictly with the rules laid down under the Transport of Animals Rules, 1978, as amended, and the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act.
I am hopeful that your esteemed Board will treat this matter with the seriousness it deserves and take prompt action to prevent further cruelty to these voiceless animals.

Thanking you in anticipation.

complaint:
I am Rajalakshmi, living in Cochin, Kerala, in a residential complex, where there are multiple houses and apartments and different Residents Welfare Associations.

I have 2 adopted indie dog children and my youngest is handicapped with weak hind legs, as she has had an accident as a puppy, which has damaged her hip and femur bones permanently. 

Twice a day I take my dogs for walking, to help nurture their health and socialising abilities. In fact, daily walking is essential for the health of my daughter, to strengthen her hind legs and the absence of it may cause more harm to her health. 

Of late there had been some issues in my complex because some residents are against me and my dogs for using the common premises including common park area for walking. There had been verbal assaults, intimidation, bullying in different occasions and these actions have created an atmosphere of hate and animosity among people, against pet animals and their parents residing here. 

Despite my repeated attempts to clarify the importance of walking for my dogs and upholding my fundamental rights as guaranteed by the law of the land, these individuals and even the RWA which represents them, has not paid any heed to it. On 5th January 2025, the said association placed a huge illegal and arbitrary warning board banning the entry of pets in the common park area to stop me from accessing that space with my children. Though I tried to contact the office bearers of this association to inform them about the illegality of this Board, which is a clear violation of your guidelines, the previous rulings of honourable High court of Kerala with regard to pet rights, they denied me any communication. 

In fact, this board has emboldened people to intimidate and isolate me even more. For instance, when I use the common roads, I am experiencing extreme hostility and implied threats from some members of this particular RWA. When I complain against it to the said association, they are saying I am instigating stuff and they will criminally implicate me in cases. They are also putting up new boards restricting entry to intimidate me. I am now left with no choice but go to the police to protect myself and complain against these injustices that I am facing, only because I am standing for the rights of voiceless animals. I am literally on the verge of abandoning my pets, because I am not able to exercise the fundamental rights guaranteed by the Constitution of India, adding to that I am not able to provide the life that my dog children, including by handicapped dog child deserves anymore. 

Hence I request you to interfere in this matter at the earliest and take necessary actions against the illegal warning board by removing it from common park area and other entry restriction boards. I also request you to take action against the said association who not only not intervened at the right moment against the hate and apathy their members openly displayed against the pets and their freedom to move,  they even went further and conspired with them to place an illegal and arbitrary board banning pets, against the laws of the land. 

I sincerely hope that the removal of the board and the actions against the association will help me and my pets live our lives without fear and harassment and in safety.
place_of_incident:
Villa 1,
Ambady Retreat,
Chilavannoor Road,
Kadavantra, Kochi 20

complaint:
Sir,
                  I am m Anandhu S. I would like to bring your kind attention towards the abnormal death of our pet dog named Kunjuttan in a private Kennel Service Named Sumiran Kennel Farm and Aquarium, Kureepuzha, Perinad, Kollam, Kerala, ph 9961414141, Gpay no9961414141.
 Kunjuttan was a Country Dog breed aged about 2 years old. We got him from road side as a stray puppy about 10 days old. He used to stay with my parents at our house in native, Kollam. Since there was some issues between my parents, father left our home and mother and Kunjuttan was alone. Now it is in legal complications between my parents. So for the safety of my mother I had no other way other than bringing my mother to my workplace Jolarpettai, Tamilnadu. Here I stay in a rented house. So for a temporary arrangement we contacted this Kennel named Sumiran Kennel and paid an amount of Rs 8000 as one month dog care fee and 1000 rupees as pick up charge.
They send a staff and brought our dog in a passenger carrying 3 wheeler Autorickshaw KL-02AW-864 for one month period on August 31, 2023. On13.09.2023 morning they called my mom and informed the dog is in serious condition due to loose motion and vomiting. And she asked them to do a video call and they denied it and said they were going hospital to treat him and on arrival they will call back. And few minutes later they informed our dog died. And prior to this there was no information from this Sumiran Kennels regarding any any health issues of our dog. 
 If we were not interested in our dog we could throw the dog to some street or we could kill it ourselves. Since he was like our family member and for us he was a little child and we had not any other option other than a kennel Service to take care of our dog. So we contacted Sumiran Kennel for a paid service. Overall after the sad demise of our pet I do not know what to do. And while they informed the death I asked them to go for legal proceedings and they denied. Then they told if required they can send body to our house. Since our house is in closed condition and I could not accept it and informed them to sepulcher and they agreed. After that totally we were in paralysed condition. And later I heard of PETA and SPCA and contacted them. From PETA and SPCA only I could find out Government Animal Welfare organisation and contact details. The Kennel did not issue any receipt for the payment. But some videos and G pay details I can submit you as proof of sending the dog and aviled their service. And those all attach here in this mail. 
   Now I realised that the kennel operator could possibly be running this place illegally and this needs to be looked into urgently as other animals they may currently have are at risk. 

       I humbly request your good office to intervene in this matter by considering  inhumanity, violence against animals and gross lawlessness going on in our society in the name as Kennel Services. And I hope it may be an eye opener to the authorities to look into the Kennel operations and pet services going on without any ethics in Kerala. If there are people like these who run such Kennel services with the sole aim of making money from dumb animals, I believe that such hidden criminals who can become a social menace will be found and brought before the law and punished according to the law.
 With the details I sent mails to support-awbi@gov.in and assistantsecretaryawbi2018@gmail.com. Here I cannot upload videos which I attached with mail. 
 Hopefully                             
Anandhu S

Sumiran Kennel farm and aquarium,

complaint: Since 2019, I am living in a Gated Community by name MUPPAS ALANKRITA in Narsingi area of Hyderabad. My RWA/OWA is not allowing me to feed stray cats in my society. Being a cat parent and animal lover, I felt very bad and hence started feeding them whenever I come across any stray cat screaming for food. Sometimes it maybe in the basement area where all cars are parked and sometimes it could be in the walkway paths. Recently one of my residents has raised one concern with a photo of a cat sitting on a car bonnet by saying... DO NOT FEED CATS IN THE APARTMENT because they leave dusty marks on cars. I strongly objected it and I reached out to my RWA/OWA seeking for any designated feeding spot to feed them. Surprisingly my RWA/OWA has denied saying, they do not allow any kind of feeding in the apartment. So, I request you to help me in setting up a dedicated feeding spot in my society to feed stray cats. I am sharing screenshots of 1.mygate helpdesk ticket wherein RWA/OWA has banned feeding in the society. 2.photo of a death cat falling from higher floors.

complaint:
I wish to bring to your attention a serious issue of animal cruelty, illegal confinement, and unlawful slaughter practices occurring at Sri Shyam Nagar, Telecom Nagar, Gachibowli, Hyderabad - 500032, near roadside shops. Goats are being continuously tied and confined in open roadside areas for prolonged periods, without proper shelter, food, or water, causing severe suffering to the animals. This also creates public nuisance, obstruction to pedestrians, foul smell, and unhygienic conditions, posing a health risk to residents.

Further, there are instances of cruel handling and illegal slaughter of animals in open/public view, which is disturbing and unlawful. As per the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960 Section 11, any act causing unnecessary pain, suffering, or cruel confinement of animals is a punishable offence. Additionally, under the Telangana Animals and Birds Sacrifices Prohibition Act, 1950, killing or sacrificing animals in public places or streets is prohibited and is a cognizable offence.

Moreover, the AP Telangana Area Slaughter of Animals Act, 1950 and Food Safety regulations require that animals be slaughtered only in licensed/approved slaughterhouses, not in open roadside areas. The Telangana Prohibition of Cow Slaughter and Animal Preservation Act, 1977 Section 6 also mandates that no animal can be slaughtered without proper certification and outside authorized premises.

Such activities clearly violate animal welfare laws, municipal sanitation rules, and public order regulations. The situation is causing distress to residents, including children, and requires urgent intervention.

I request the concerned authorities to conduct an immediate inspection, stop the illegal confinement of animals on roads, take strict action against those involved in illegal slaughter, and ensure compliance with all applicable animal welfare and public health laws.
place_of_incident: Sri Shyam Nagar, Telecom Nagar, Gachibowli, Hyderabad, Telangana 500032

complaint: Hi Sir, Subject: Complaint Regarding Attempt to Kidnap and Kill Pet Cats by Neighbours Through Hired Rowdies. Details of the Incident: On Date- 13/08/2025 and Time between 1:45-2:30. I saw a shadow pass by my window, and upon inspection, I found a man had entered the common space on the first floor of our apartment building. This individual was clearly attempting to kidnap my pet cat, who was sitting in the balcony area, along with another community cat we feed and care for regularly. When I confronted the individual, he fled the scene. I immediately followed and saw that he joined four other men who were waiting below. In total, five rowdies had gathered to execute this cruel and unlawful act. Upon inquiry and based on previous threats and behavior, I have strong reason to believe that this act was planned, financed, and ordered by our neighbours, who have openly expressed discontent about the presence of cats in the area but never made any formal complaint or communicated their issue with us. Legal Violations and Harassment- 1. Section 509 of IPC As a woman who is being threatened and harassed for showing kindness to animals. I seek protection under this section against verbal abuse and mental trauma caused by our neighbours continued harassment. 2. Section 11 of The Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960 -This act criminalizes the displacement or harm to animals and their act of attempting to kidnap and likely harm the cats directly violates this law. 3. Article 51A g and Article 19 of the Indian Constitution - I have the constitutional right to compassion for living creatures and to continue any lawful occupation ncluding caring for animals.The neighbours attempts to deny me this right amount to a constitutional violation. 4.The Theft Act 1968 Section 4.1- A cat is considered property under this law, and this attempted kidnapping constitutes theft. 5. Section 503 IPC - Criminal intimidation is clearly applicable here, as this incident was not isolated but part of a pattern of ongoing threats and harassment by our neighbours. Request for Action: I kindly request you to take immediate legal action against the individuals involved, including: 1. The five unknown rowdies who trespassed into our premises with the intention to kidnap the pet and kill/harm it. 2. Our neighbours who are suspected of hiring these individuals, and who have been mentally harassing me and my family for caring for animals, without ever communicating their grievances lawfully. I urge you to register an F.I.R. under the appropriate sections listed above, investigate the matter thoroughly, and ensure that no further harm is caused to the animals or to my family. Thank you, Name- A.Mary Manasa Charmila Add- G-5 SSV Yadadri Towers ,Nizampet village,500090,HYD Phone- 8989875414

In addition to the suspected neglect, the dogs frequently bark together, both during the day and at night, creating a highly unpleasant and disruptive atmosphere in the neighborhood. The noise levels have become a serious concern for residents, affecting peace, sleep, and overall quality of life.

This situation appears to violate the principles laid out in the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960, which mandates that animals must not be subjected to unnecessary pain or suffering. Prolonged confinement, lack of stimulation, and isolation can lead to severe distress and health issues in dogs. Furthermore, the persistent noise disturbance may also fall under public nuisance as defined by local municipal regulations.

We respectfully request that the concerned authorities: 1. Conduct an immediate inspection of the premises. 2. Ensure that the animals are being kept in humane and sanitary conditions. 3. Take appropriate action if any violations of animal welfare or public nuisance laws are found.

complaint:
Respected sir/madam

My name is Lavanya Mannepally, wife of Raj Tarun Nidamarthi, an actor in the Telugu film industry. I am writing to you with deep pain and concern to report a serious case of animal cruelty, neglect, and violence committed by Mr. Raj Tarun and his associates.
I have been living in our home with our dogs for the past 12 years since 2013 Raj tharun began collecting dogs of various breeds starting with two Pomeranians and later bringing home more dogs including a Labrador Retriever, two Beagles, an English Pointer, a German Shepherd, and many others, totaling around 20 dogs Over time, he abandoned their care, and I took full responsibility for them. 
Rajtarun abandoned animals for his extramarital affair with actress malvi Malhotra who is allergic to dogs and Raj tarun is also allergic now .
In 2024, due to marital disputes, Raj Tarun left the house and stopped providing any financial support. As a homemaker who was dependent on him, I struggled to survive. With the help of my father, Mr. Mannepally Ramarao, I continued to take care of our senior dogs, most of whom are now aged and in need of special care.
Despite several requests, Raj Tarun refused to help. On the night of May 17, 2025, he and a group of men forcibly entered the house, attacked me, and threw me out along with 15 elderly dogs in the middle of the night. One of the dogs, aged 11, suffered a fractured leg during the incident as they were cruelly dragged and thrown out. We were left on the street, homeless and helpless.
This act of cruelty and violence was done purely out of greed for property. Raj Tarun must be held accountable for this inhumane act. These dogs, who were once brought home by him, have been treated as disposable lives.
By the grace of God, a kind woman named Mrs. Vasantha gave temporary shelter to me and the dogs that night. I have filed a complaint with the local police in Hyderabad, and I am attaching some evidences , photos/videos/documents  with this email to support my case.
I humbly request the Animal Welfare Board of India to take immediate and strict action against Mr. Raj Tarun and those involved. Please help us get justice and protect these innocent animals who have suffered immensely.

Sincerely,
Lavanya Mannepally
W/o Raj Tarun Nidamarthi
Villa no 13, pavani boulevard,
Kokapet, 500075.

complaint: pls.see attachments

I am writing to lodge a formal complaint against certain residents of our locality who have repeatedly engaged in unlawful and cruel acts against a family of street dogs. This is not an isolated incident but the third instance of such conduct, demonstrating a continuing pattern of deliberate mistreatment.
The present matter concerns a female street dog who has been relocated twice earlier and has, on both occasions, returned to her original territory. She recently gave birth to three puppies, all of whom are approximately three months old. Both the mother and the puppies are vaccinated, and there have been no incidents of aggression or harm caused by them to any resident.
Despite this, the individuals in question have once again orchestrated the removal of this dog family. It is important to note that such relocation is illegal under the Animal Birth Control Rules, 2001 and violates the protections guaranteed under the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960. Street dogs cannot be arbitrarily relocated, especially when they are vaccinated, sterilized, and non-aggressive.
Further, the conduct of these residents has not been limited to relocation. There have been repeated acts of cruelty, including attempts to run over the dogs with vehicles, beating them with sticks, and creating an environment of fear and violence. These actions are in direct contravention of statutory protections afforded to animals and constitute punishable offences.
It has also come to light that the person who initiated this relocation has paid private individuals to forcibly remove the dogs with explicit instructions not to return them to their original location. This deliberate attempt to permanently displace the animals reflects a clear intention to bypass the law and continue harassment.
place_of_incident: Ward no.10, Babu Ram Kishan Ki Bagichi, Near S.D Modern School, Panipat, Haryana 132103

complaint:
Urgent Concern: Illegal Relocation of Stray Dogs Against Supreme Court Guidelines

Repected Committee Members/Concerned Authorities, 

I hope this message finds you well.

I write to you with a heavy heart and deep concern regarding the recent and unlawful relocation of stray dogs from our society premises. These animals, who have lived peacefully among us for years, have been suddenly removed an act not only heartbreaking but also in direct violation of the Honourable Supreme Court guidelines.

It is important to remember that the Supreme Court of India, through multiple judgments, has clearly mandated that stray dogs cannot be relocated or removed forcefully from their territories unless under specific circumstances laid out by law. These rulings are grounded in compassion, the constitutional right of every living being to exist, and the importance of co-existence in a humane society.

Furthermore, let us reflect on the fact that not a single case of dog biting has been reported in our society. These animals have shown no aggression, and many residents, including children and the elderly, have coexisted with them without fear or discomfort. Their sudden disappearance not only disrupts this balance but may invite more unsterilized dogs from outside to enter, undoing years of peaceful coexistence.

We urge the concerned individuals or committee to kindly look into this matter with empathy and urgency. Let us align our actions with the law and our shared values of compassion and responsibility.

We request that immediate steps be taken to trace and return the dogs to their rightful territory, and to ensure that such actions are not repeated in the future. A humane and lawful approach is the only path forward.

Thank you for understanding and standing up for what is both legally and morally right.

Warm regards,
Sourav Katoch,
Resident Greencourt Society,
Mobile number 9599956097
place_of_incident: Greencourt Society, Sector 90, Gurgaon, Haryana, 122505

complaint: A 5-6 months old puppy was beaten so hard that it almost died The back is broken and is unable to walk.

The accused is my neighbor. This complaint is lodged by Chelsea Panchal under the guardianship of Neeru Panchal, currently residing at 555A, Sec-2, Bahadurgarh, Haryana. The accused is the resident of sector 2 as well, currently living on rent nearby; their plot resides directly facing our house which is house no. 608, which now they are currently building. First situation: During late winter, the accused was bitten by a street dog. The reason was not clear why the dog bit her, but as soon as the dog bit her, the dog ran with its tail tucked under its legs. No one in the area knew why the dog bit her. Some of the dogs here are neutered and vaccinated as per the old residents. The accused was again bitten by another dog of the area again for no apparent reason, as per her statement; like the previous time, she blamed us for giving food to them, which is less of a concern. But as per her response to the first situation, she started carrying a thick piece of wood to scare them in her defense and started hitting them with small stones. In response to the second situation, she called her nephew, who is a minor and throws bricks and beats every dog with thick wooden sticks they find in their way. The accused has gotten an oral warning from our side many times to immediately discontinue this act of violence towards the street dogs, but the accused person is hiding behind her nephew; we have tried talking to each member of their family, and each of them thinks that this act of violence is right. The concern is that they follow an act of violence each time they see any dog on the street. Right now they abuse them when they have not fully shifted in their under-construction house. What will happen when they permanently reside here? According to the accused, two different dogs bit her, for no clear reason, as per her. All the dogs here have lived here before us, and to abuse them and to kick them out of their territory is an unlawful act. We have lived here for almost 2.5 years, and hundreds of unknown Swiggy, Zomato, and Blinkit people have come in front of our house; none of them have once gotten bitten. The children of our area also play with them; the dogs never showed any aggression or even growled. The point is that the accused is blaming the street dogs and performing an act of violence towards them directly and indirectly. Each time we sit in our house and they come to check their plot, we hear voices of dogs being hit with heavy objects. The dogs they hit were still adult dogs 2.5 years ago when we came into this neighborhood, unaware of their age, but the neighbors approximation is that the dogs have entered the senior dog category. The dogs are not aggressive yet, but if they keep on hitting them, the day is not very far off that they become aggressive.
Hence, the people in the area suggested the accused give street dogs something to eat or told her to carry a big stick to scare them off. She never tried offering them food or anything but straightforwardly opted for violence in response to the second situation. None of the neighbors or any person living nearby suggested her to actually beat them or forcefully push them out from anywhere near her house.
I am traumatized to see such actions of the accused; the act of violence by her and her nephew are outrageous and affect the essence of the society deeply. I beg the AWBI to take measurable and righteous actions so them beings do not suffer any longer violence from the accused in her actions of revenge or grudges she held from the above two situations.
place_of_incident: H.NO-608 ,SECTOR-2 ,BAHADURGARH ,HARYANA
