She was aggressive in nature so sometimes it became difficult for us to manage her, so we decided to take her to a Shelter Home for Dogs. On the recommendation of few known people in the Metro Pet Hospital, Sector-23, Gurgaon, Haryana, where we often used to go for Oreos boarding and treatment, we contacted Mr. Saptarshi Roy, contact no. 9958462494/8766399239. He is the founder of Safe House for Animals Care and Compassion Shelter, Near Sultanpur, Gurgaon Farrukh Nagar Road, Village Sadhrana, Haryana-122505.
Then Mr. Saptarshi Roy asked us to send Rs. 25,000 as a One Time Fees for Oreos Lifetime Care at his Shelter Home. We handed over our Dear Pet Dog Oreo in the shelter on 4th December, 2023. He regularly used to send us videos of Oreo eating food in shelters on random days whenever it was requested by us. Then for a week he went incommunicado and suddenly on 27th December, 2023 at 17.38 Mr. Saptarshi Roy suddenly sent us the blood test report of Oreo which clearly indicates her health has deteriorated very badly indicating Multiple Organ Failure as if blood test was done on Oreo at her death bed. But when we did a blood test in 2022, that is 1 year back, the reports came out to be completely normal. When we called him and said we would like to bring Oreo back to our Homes for treatment, but he said there is nothing to panic about and this is very normal for a Pet Dog while adjusting to new surroundings in the Shelter Home and medication is also provided to Oreo and she will recover soon. But The very next day on i.e., 28th December, 2023, at around 11.06 in the morning he sent us two pictures of Oreo lying unconsciously and being given medication drips.
We were very worried to which Mr. Saptarshi Roy replied in a very casual and inconsiderate tone saying that he will send back Oreo to our house by evening if we dont trust him in an ambulance at his own cost. We agreed to it. But in the evening, he informed us that his Ambulance had a Tire Puncture and his Father has died so he is leaving for some Hospital in Kolkata and will not be available on his phone today. Then at night, he again messaged me saying that Oreo will be sent tomorrow morning by 8.30 a.m. But in the middle of the night at 1.53 am the next day on 29th December, 2023, he messaged us saying that The Kid Oreo passed away just came to know.
We saw the message at around 8.30 am and were completely heartbroken and soon me and my husband rushed to his Shelter Home in Village Sadhrana. But by that time they had already buried Oreo but on our request, they dug up her grave for us to see Oreo for one last time and to do the last final rites. But when we enquired the people in his Shelter Home they told us that our Dog Oreo had died at 11.00 pm itself and that they have sent a picture of Oreo also to Mr. Saptarshi Roy. Then he last contacted us at 10: 30 am saying he will talk to us in the evening as he is going to immerse the ashes of his father and he is going to Gaya. But ever since then neither has he called back nor has answered our questions nor has he sent us the last pictures of Dear Oreo Dog and is since then he is also not taking our calls at all and neither has he returned the money Rs.25,000 taken from me at the time of Boarding of Oreo, which he promised that he will return after deducting some charges. He has also now blocked us entirely on his WhatsApp Chats and calls.
We enquired from Oreos Doctor, Dr. Satbir Josan, Founder of Metro Pet Hospital, to which he said that they only received the blood test report, and Oreo was never ever brought to the clinic and the Blood Test Reports indicate Kidney Failure. Mr. Saptarshi Roy is also a frequent visitor of the same hospital for treating ill Dogs at his shelter house.
For us, this whole story of Oreo falling ill suddenly and passing away in just one day seems to be illogical and has been cooked up entirely. We suspect foul play in our Pet Dog Oreos Unnatural Death by way of beating. Kindly Please help us bring the truth to light the actual cause of Oreo Unnatural Death and also help us retrieve the money given by us to give Justice to my Dear Pet Dog Oreo which underwent such a trauma at his Shelter that it passed away within 24 days of handing over to his Shelter Home.
Now what we now need is an Unconditional Apology and Adequate amount of Compensation is also Required for Causing Unbearable Mental Agony for the past 1 month to me and my family and for Unnatural Killing of our Healthy Pet Dog Namely Oreo is Required from Mr. Saptarshi Roy Founder of Safe House for Animals. When we went to the shelter for first time, there were more than hundred dogs. But on our last visit to do last rites of our beloved Oreo, we hardly saw 15 dogs in the shelter. We suspect mass killing of animals in that shelter too. We have lodged police complaint but no action taken.
Regards,
Sridevi M.
place_of_incident: Safe House for Animals Care and Compassion Shelter, Village Sadhrana, Farrukhnagar Road, Gurgaon, Haryana-122505

complaint:
Respected Sir/Madam,
I wish to file a formal complaint against the arbitrary and unlawful actions of the management of Runwal Forests premises in restricting entry of an external feeder engaged by me for feeding community animals residing within the premises.
1.	I am a lawful resident and member of the society and have been undertaking feeding, vaccination, sterlisation and medical help of 20 community dogs within the complex out of compassion and responsibility.
2.	Due to my professional commitments, I have appointed my staff as feeder to carry out this activity. However, the management has proposed to restrict entry of such staff, which is arbitrary and discriminatory.
3. The society continues to allow entry of other external personnel such as maids, drivers, housekeeping staff, and delivery agents. Selective restriction only in this case is unjustified and violative of my rights as a member.
4. As per the ABC rule, it is the responsibility of the Resident Welfare Association / management to ensure that community animals are provided with food and water. In the present case, this responsibility lies with the the managing bodies of Runwal Forests, namely Runwal Forests Tower 1, 2, 3, 4 CHSL; Runwal Forests Tower 5, 6, 7 CHSL; Runwal Forests Tower 8 CHSL; Runwal Forest Tower 9 CHSL; along with the builder/overall management but these managing commitees and Runwal facility management team do not take any such responsilibity.
5. I have been voluntarily fulfilling this responsibility. If the society refuses entry to my appointed feeder, then all the 20 dogs residing within the premises would stay hungry as I am the only care taker within the complex.
6.	Denial of food to community animals amounts to cruelty under Section 11 of the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960 and is a punishable offence. Further, obstruction or harassment of individuals engaged in feeding community animals is also actionable under law and relevant municipal guidelines.
7.	The present action of the society is not only in violation of statutory provisions but also creates risk of animal distress, which may lead to avoidable incidents within the premises.

Relief Sought:
I respectfully request your office to:
1.	Direct the society management to permit entry of the feeder appointed by me for carrying out feeding of community animals; OR
2.	In the alternative, direct the society to take full responsibility for feeding, medical care, and welfare of all community animals within the premises in compliance with applicable laws
3.	Issue necessary directions to prevent harassment or obstruction in lawful feeding activities

I request your urgent intervention in this matter to ensure compliance with law and to prevent cruelty to animals. Please find attached email from the team that has given ultimatum till 21-04-2026 further which there will be no feeding in the complex.
place_of_incident: Runwal forests, LBS Marg, kanjurmarg west Mumbai

I wish to file a complaint against a pet shop operating without a valid registration /license under the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Pet ShopRules, 2018,
The shop is causing serious animal welfare and public health concerns by keeping birds in cages on the society pathway, exposed directly to air, harsh sunlight, and heavy rains, especially during the current heavy rainfall season. This neglectful practice causes unnecessary suffering and stress to the animals, which amounts to cruelty under Sections 3 and 11 of the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960.
Additionally, the shop disposes of dead or unwanted fishes by throwing them across the footpath onto trees and other public areas. This practice is unhygienic, environmentally harmful, and creates a biohazard, violating animal welfare and municipal cleanliness regulations.

I request urgent intervention to inspect the pet shop, cancel any illegal operation, remove animals from cruel conditions, and enforce penalties as per the law.
Attached are photos/videos as evidence.
Thank you for your immediate attention to this serious matter.

complaint:
Subject: Formal Complaint Regarding Harassment and Unreasonable Demands from Neighbour-SHANKAR BABAN LOHOT resident of F-505 F Building, Pride World City, Charholi Budruk, Pin-412105, Pune, Maharashtra.

Dear Sir/Madam,
I am writing to file a formal complaint concerning ongoing harassment and disruptive behaviour from my neighbour residing at F-505 F-Building, Pride World City, Charholi Budruk. 
His behaviour has created a hostile living environment and has escalated to include unreasonable demands regarding my pet.
1. Description of Harassment:
For the past couple of days, he has been engaged in a pattern of behaviour intended to intimidate and harass myself and my household. This includes:
Excessive and Malicious Complaints: He has been reporting false acquisitions about me in society and trying to impose his decisions to put muzzle on my dog despite there is no such rules as per society bylaws made aware to me.
Unwarranted Confrontation: He and his family members have accosted me and on multiple occasions over phone calls and common areas, using aggressive and accusatory language.

2. The Unreasonable Demand:
Most recently, on 20th Sept 7:30pm He asked me to meet at main gate of the society and started threatening me by intimidating and causing intentional insult with intent to provoke a breach of the peace. 
On 21st Sept from 10pm onwards He has made numerous threatening calls to me along with his brothers to meet me in person again. They threatened to take matters into their own hands if I did not comply.
My dog, Buzo, is a Golden Retriever who is fully vaccinated, and well-trained. He is not aggressive and has never threatened or harmed any person or animal. The demand to muzzle a non-aggressive dog on its own property is unreasonable, unjustified, and constitutes an attempt to impose their own rules upon me.
3. Why This is Unacceptable:
Lack of Justification: There is no legal or behavioral basis for this demand. My dog is not a public nuisance or a danger.
Harassment: This demand is the latest escalation in a campaign of harassment designed to force us to comply with their unreasonable standards or to drive us from our home.
Animal Welfare: Muzzling a dog for extended periods, is an animal welfare concern. It can prevent panting; essential for temperature regulation, drinking, and can cause significant distress to the animal.
4. To help resolve this I have attempted to be a reasonable neighbour have:
a. Ensured my dog is not left outside unattended for long periods.
b. Attempted to speak with the neighbours calmly to address their concerns, but these attempts have been met with hostility.

5. My Request for Action:
a.	This pattern of harassment and intimidation cannot continue. I request that you:
b.	Speak with the residents SHANKAR BABAN LOHOT at F-505 F Building, Pride World City, Charholi Budruk and inform them that their harassment must cease immediately.
c.	Advise them that their demand for a muzzle is unreasonable and without merit.
d.	Issue a formal warning to them regarding their behaviour, noting that further harassment may result in fines from the HOA / lease violation penalties from management / police involvement.
e.	Document this complaint in both of our files.

I have kept a detailed log of these incidents, including dates, times, and the nature of each encounter, which I can provide upon request. I am also prepared to provide character references for my dog from trainers or neighbours.

I expect to live in my home peacefully and without harassment. Thank you for your prompt attention to this serious matter.

Regards,
Ajay Kumar Singh
Cell: 91-7276072607
email: aj.aks360@gmail.com
Flat-1302, F wing, Pride World City, Charholi Budruk, Pune, MH.

complaint:
Dear Sir, Madam, Team,

There are multiple stray cows wondering in the city approximately more than 100. Multiple request were made by the people to the Chief Officer, Nagar Parishad Pusad to arrange for their food, water shelter however no arrangement are made by the Chief Officer since many years. Further, request was also made to shift the stray cows and calves to government registered Gaushalas however, the authority is reluctant to take any action for shifting the stray cows and calfves to the Gaushalas for their safety.  Even the Gaushalas are ready to accept all the cows and calves and a letter to that effect is already provided to the CO, Municipal Council.  Due to their presence on roads they are prone to accidents causing health risks and life risks.  These cows and calves relay on waste foods from houses, hotels and markets.  They consume plastics and that heavily affects their health.  Most of the cows deliver calves on the road sides and the new born calf has to live his life on roads from Day 1 without any medical or social assistance.  Further, the city has only one pound for animals which is located on one end of the city and that makes it practically impossible for people to transfer these stray in the pounds.   

The Municipal Council has neither taken any steps to find the owners of the cows and calves, nor they has made any arrangement for the animals and accordingly they has completely failed in performance of their duties.

Accordingly, we request you to strictly direct the chief officer, Municipal Council Pusad to follow all the instructions issued by AWBI and the directions issued by Supreme Court vide Advisory No 6-3/2019-20 Advisory dated 27-02-2020 AND Advisory No 9-3/2018-19/PCA dated 12 July 2018, in all the letter and spirit.  

We have enclosed herewith all the photos of the stray cows calves living on the streets of Pusad city.  The photos clearly shows the time, date, address and GPS locations of the photos.

Thus strict action is required to be taken against the accused as they are clearly ignoring the AWBI advisories and Supreme Court directions.
place_of_incident: MAHATMA GANDHI CHOWK, PUSAD

complaint:
Subject: Urgent Concern Regarding Horse Cart Activity Outside Veer Savarkar Garden, TPS Road

Respected Sir/Madam,

I am writing to express my deep concern and frustration regarding the horse cart rides taking place outside Veer Savarkar Garden on TPS Road. As a member of this community and someone who cares deeply about animal welfare, I am appalled by the sight of these horse-drawn carts carrying a group of people, including children and parents, at a time.

The carts, which regularly hold 4-5 people along with the driver, appear to be causing immense strain on the horse. Watching the animal, clearly overburdened with the weight it is forced to carry, is deeply distressing. It is heartbreaking to see such a majestic creature being used as a mere tool for entertainment at the expense of its health and well-being.

The horse is an intelligent and sensitive animal, capable of feeling pain and stress. The constant pulling of carts, often carrying excessive weight, in what I believe is harsh weather or unfavorable conditions, is nothing short of exploitation. It is clear that these animals are being subjected to forced labor, simply for the sake of providing amusement for the public. This situation is both an emotional and moral violation that I cannot ignore.

I urge the concerned authorities to intervene and take immediate action to stop this inhumane practice. It is vital that a formal investigation be conducted into the conditions under which these horses are kept, the load they are forced to carry, and whether their well-being is being properly considered. I would like to know if there is any way that I, as a concerned citizen, can contribute to halting this cruelty or report it further for the benefit of these animals.

It is high time we as a society take a stand against such cruelty disguised as entertainment, and I sincerely hope that action will be taken swiftly. I am hopeful that your office or the relevant animal welfare agency can work together to put an end to this practice, ensuring that the animals involved are treated with the respect, care, and kindness they deserve.

Thank you for your attention to this pressing issue. I look forward to your response and any steps that can be taken to protect these horses from further harm.

Sincerely,
Shruti Shah
9833311773
Borivali West, Mumbai

complaint: I, Prajwal Kamlesh Dube residing at house number 465/2603, Sant Tukaram Nagar Pimpri 411018. I am compelled to bring to attention the unjust closure of my complaint by police dated 20th December 2024. My initial grievance to Pimpri Chinchwad Police Commissioner highlighted the brutal assault of a stray dog, the intimidation and unlawful assembly outside my residence, by the tenant of House No. 464/2598. My complaint was closed by the Senior Police Inspector Gorakh Kumbhar on false statement given by the accused. Even though I have a CCTV footages to support my grievance. The closure of the case based solely on the statement of a non-complainant is not only disappointing but also raises serious concerns about the impartiality of the investigation.On December 20, 2024, at approximately 7:51 PM, I witnessed a disturbing and unwarranted act of animal cruelty in my neighborhood. Two tenants residing at House No. 464/2598 viciously attacked a stray dog using heavy tiles, hitting the animal multiple times on its back. The attack left the dog severely injured, bleeding from the leg, later unable to walk. Disturbed by this incident, I immediately called the police at 7:53 PM to report the matter and provided details, including the nature of the injuries and the location.In response to my call, two police officers promptly arrived at the scene. However, one of the culprits had fled before their arrival, and the other was taken into custody and subsequently brought to YCM Hospital. At that time, I chose not to escalate the matter further, assuming that the law would handle the situation appropriately. Later that evening, at approximately 11:00 PM, my mother observed a group of individuals, including the culprits roomates and three unknown persons gathering suspiciously near my house.When my mother opened the door to check the situation, they moved towards the room of the accused.I stepped out to assess the situation and noticed five individuals standing nearby. As I passed them, one of them approached me and verbally threatened me, saying that dont file the complaint againt.If you do, we have already spoken to the police, and they have told us to just say that we hit the dog in self-defense. When I ignored his threats and continued walking, he further intimidated me claiming that the police are with them. Such blatant attempts to coerce and intimidate me and my family were alarming. Despite their intimidation, I refrained from any confrontation and trusted that my complaint to the police would ensure justice. Later I learned that the tenants along with Mr Rajan Guhagar, Kartik Wagh and others have decided to kill the dog. I also have a call recording of Mr Rajan Guhagar who threatened me that he would have killed the dog if he was young enough to run behind the dog. Mr Rajan Guhagar had also threatened me in front of police on 20/12/2024 that he would kill the dog if dog is not taken away by municipal corporation. I made three different complaints based on the evidence I had. Unfortunately, I later learned that the police had recorded a statement from a non complainant, Mr Rajan Guhagar and Ajit Rajnikant Makasare, who claimed that the dog attacked him, forcing him to defend himself by throwing a stone. His statement further alleged that my family threatened him to vacate the premises. These claims are not only fabricated but also appear to be made under the influence of the accused individuals to mislead the investigation and protect the perpetrators. The non complainants statement also falsely accuses my family of repeatedly filing frivolous complaints. Such baseless allegations are deeply distressing and divert attention from the original offense of animal cruelty. It is also evident that the police failed to consider my eyewitness account and CCTV footages, the severity of the dogs injuries, and the intimidation faced by my family, raising concerns about bias in the investigation. The accused has got the support of Senior Police Inspector of Sant Tukaram Nagar Police Station, Assistant Sub Inspector Deepak Mahajan who helped the accused to record such statement so that my complaint can be closed. They also falsely narrated my incident to Deputy Commissioner Swapna Gore who in support gave letter to close my complaint.

complaint: The newly appointed management of our residential complex has designated the society garbage collection storage place as the feeding area for all the cats. And the feeder has to strictly adhere to this decision. This decision has led to a multitude of challenges, which I believe need immediate attention and resolution. This decision is disappointing, highly inappropriate and poses severe issues affecting the safety and overall well-being of the cats. Firstly, the allocated area is highly unsanitary, unhygienic and unsuitable for the proper feeding and nourishment of the cats. The cracked roof makes it impossible to shelter them adequately during the rainy season, exposing them and me to rains and jeopardising their health and nutrition, as well as my health. Moreover, the absence of a clean surface further hinders their access to food and leads to an unhygienic feeding environment. Additionally, the proximity of the feeding spot to the main road raises serious concerns. The constant presence of vehicles passing by generates loud noise pollution, which causes distress and fear among the cats. This exacerbates their sensitivity and prevents them from feeling safe while approaching the feeding area. Furthermore, the presence of stray dogs in the vicinity adds to the fear and anxiety experienced by the cats, making it challenging to gather them in one specific spot as cats are territorial animals and unlikely to leave their established areas voluntarily. Hence, designating feeding spots that respects their territorial nature and safety is crucial. All the cats, due to their nature and territorial behavior, do not follow me to the new allotted garbage collection feeding spot where the majority of the residents and management would prefer me to feed them. Consequently, I am left with no choice but to feed them in an isolated area close to their territory, where they feel safe. For this I am forced to go to feed the cats at midnight. In contrast, those who harbor disdain for animals are free to roam anytime without facing any repercussions. I am not allowed to keep a bowl of clean water in my own closed parking area or anywhere within society premises, as per the society management.  Feeding stray animals is not a crime, and it is a basic right of animals to have access to food and water. Animal feeding rights are protected by law, yet I have to resort to feeding them in secret at night because of the unjustified hatred that residents have towards me and the community cats. The harassment and discrimination I face for carrying out this noble cause of feeding not only affects my mental well-being but also jeopardizes my physical health. The lack of sleep, due to the need to go out at midnight, has compromised my overall health and well-being. Additionally, the cats are left starving throughout the day when they could be benefitting from regular nourishment. Earlier I fed twice a day.It is crucial to acknowledge that the feeder, such as myself, should have the authority to select appropriate feeding spots, as per the cats territorial areas within the society. That meets the specific needs and safety requirements of the cats. I kindly request your immediate attention to rectify this issue by considering a more suitable and hygienic location for the cats feeding station within the premises. The notice alleges that I am not using the designated feeding area allocated by the society and, in doing so, I am violating society norms. the feeding area designated by the management is the Society garbage collection area situated at main road, is filthy, unhygience and all cats being territorial in nature, dont leave their specific area. Its challenging and impossible to gather all cats together to the allocated garbage area to feed. The cat population within the society has not increased in fact, it has decreased due to successful sterilisation efforts. Kittens found within the society are often abandoned, and I have taken responsibility for their foster care. And also, due fatal injuries inflicted by residents on stray community cats. The behavior of cats, including scratching and defecation, is natural and beyond the control of feeders. Cats are generally shy and avoid humans, and it is often children who provoke or harass them for amusement. The notice threatens to impose fines on me for any instances of littering, damage, or violations attributed to society cats, which will be added to my maintenance bill according to the societys guidelines. This punitive approach is both unfair and unjust, illegal, given that feeders do not have control over the natural behavior of cats. I kindly request your intervention in this matter to ensure that justice is served, and my efforts to care for the community cats are not hindered by unfounded accusations and harassment. Thank You.
place_of_incident:
Lunkad Goldcoast Society. Next to Symbiosis International school of Design,
Viman-Nagar.

complaint:
Attempt to relocate community cats under the false pretext of sterilisation and false allegation, on August 23rd, 2023, at approximately 11 A.M. Mr. Pawan Sharma, a resident and joint secretary of society Lunkad Goldcoast, along with his wife, Mrs. Deepika Sharma, attempted to relocate community cats under the pretext of sterilising them, although all cats are sterilised. They sought the assistance of the Universal Animal Welfare Society, Pune for this purpose, without any proper authority or legal process. On the aforementioned date and time, a van with the registration number MH12 SF 5666 entered society premises with the approval of Mrs. Deepika Sharma. The vehicle parked in front of her block, building D2. I was alerted and discovered that two individuals, Mayur and his partner, from the same van were attempting to catch the community cats. I immediately approached and questioned the individuals involved to ascertain the purpose behind their actions. Unfortunately, by the time I reached, these two guys had already trapped a kitten in a cage, Society CCTV clip available. On questioning why he had trapped a tiny 1-2 months young kitten, Mayur replied, he trapped the kitten, to gain adult cats trust and to trap them as well. All residents and management of Lunkad goldcoast are aware of the fact, all the community cats are sterilised  and vaccinated. Mayur and his partner failed to show any document or identification card and attempted to relocate all cats that have already been sterilized and vaccinated. Claiming to provide sterilization services, without verifying their ears. Ears of all the community cats are notched a clear indicator of sterilization,  The mentioned van had many cages inside, clearly he came with an intention to trap all the cats and relocate. Further, when Mayur felt a lack of excuse to pick the cats, he had a telephonic discussion with Mrs. Deepika Sharma.  Mayur informed Deepika Sharma is complaining about her daughter being bitten by a stray cat. I firmly believe that these allegations are baseless and unjust, as they go against the natural behavior of cats.  As you are aware, cats, especially stray cats, are naturally shy and tend to run away when approached by humans. It is highly implausible that a stray cat would initiate an unprovoked attack on a person, especially a child, unless it felt threatened or provoked. The assertion that a cat suddenly emerged and bit her daughter seems highly improbable. I have witnessed and received reports from various concerned neighbours about incidents where both children and adults have been intentionally troubling these defenseless animals. Their actions include kicking the cats, pulling tail harshly, trapping in garbage bins, poking with stick and engaging in other forms of harassment while the cats are peacefully resting or sitting quietly. Residents then proceed to make false accusations of the cats biting and attacking them. It is evident that these claims are fabricated to justify their mistreatment and to divert attention from their own inappropriate behavior towards these vulnerable creatures. Its a false accusation by Mrs. Deepika Sharma, in an attempt to falsely defame the stray cats, and me. On the grounds of such false accusation the Universal van without any investigation, authority, decided to trap all society cats and to relocate them. This act of removing the cats on false grounds of alleged cat bites is unjust, inhumane and is absolutely illegal. f I come to know of his presence in the society, not only I but all security guards will join me, intervene and become a hurdle. He said it in the presence of Secretary of society, Mr. Prashant Kamath.
Such a baseless insinuation is absolutely disrespectful, disheartening. It is extremely unfair and unjust for Mrs. Sharma to pass judgment on my character, based on my marital status, as I am single. As an advocate for equality, I firmly believe in treating all individuals with respect, regardless of their occupation or background. I have consistently engaged in friendly conversations with the housekeeping staff, maids, and security guards, as I believe in fostering a sense of community within our society. I believe that it is crucial to address this incident promptly and take necessary action to protect the well-being of the community cats. Video recording of Mayur act of trapping kitten and his explanation, is available. Contact details : Mrs. Deepika Sharma - 9762392828 , Mr. Pawan Sharma - 9860200284 , residents in D2 - 201, Lunkad Goldcoast Society. Secretary Mr Prashant Kamath 9769161051. Universal Animal Welfare Society, Pune, employee, Mr. Mayur 7767996665. 
Please copy and paste in web browser to watch evidence videos,  https://photos.app.goo.gl/GccCQfEtYACW4N3BA
Request you take appropriate actions against this illegal activity of relocating sterilised cats. A complaint of the same was raised and mailed to support-awbi@gov.in, daed Sept 21, 2023. Thank you .

Due to this denial of care, the animal continued to suffer until I arranged for a private veterinarian at my own cost. The refusal of medical attention to a sick cow is not only negligence but deliberate cruelty. Further, medicines are never available in the hospital. Villagers are always forced to purchase medicines at high prices from the local market. The repeated failure to provide treatment and medicines is causing continuous suffering to animals in this area and nearby villages.

complaint:
I am submitting this complaint to report serious and repeated acts of cruelty against stray puppies in my locality and the complete negligence of the guardians of a minor responsible for these acts.

I am taking care of six stray puppies in my area. These puppies are harmless and non aggressive and do not pose any threat to residents. Stray animals are protected under Indian law and citizens are legally permitted to feed and care for them.

Despite this, a neighbors minor child has been repeatedly and deliberately abusing these puppies by kicking them forcefully. This behavior is intentional and violent and not accidental or playful. The child has consistently targeted the puppies in sensitive areas causing visible pain fear and distress.

On 03-01-2026 in the morning the child kicked one of the puppies forcefully in the abdominal area. The impact was so severe that the puppy was thrown backward and shortly after started vomiting continuously. This is a clear indication of internal injury or severe trauma and could have resulted in death.

I immediately approached the parents of the child and requested them to stop this cruelty. However they refused to intervene and showed complete disregard for the seriousness of the act. Their response reflected negligence irresponsibility and a lack of basic compassion.

These acts clearly fall under cruelty to animals as defined under Section 11 of the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act 1960 which prohibits beating kicking or causing unnecessary pain or suffering to any animal. Guardians are responsible for controlling the actions of minors under their supervision and failure to do so amounts to negligence.

The continued violence against these puppies places their lives at constant risk and has caused me extreme distress as a responsible citizen who is trying to protect vulnerable animals. Such behavior if ignored encourages cruelty and poses a serious concern for public safety and moral responsibility.

I strongly request the Animal Welfare Board of India to take immediate action in this matter direct the appropriate authorities to intervene and ensure the safety and protection of the stray puppies so that no further harm is caused.

I declare that the information stated above is true to the best of my knowledge and belief and is submitted in good faith.
place_of_incident: Dipchand Math, Sheoraphuli, Hooghly

complaint: I purchased this Labrador from a seller at the Gallif Street Pet Market for Rs. 8500 on 9th March at 10 am. At the time of sale the shopkeeper said that the dog was 40 days old, no vaccination has been done as the vaccination starts from 45 days of age, which is true. He asked me not to feed anything to the dog till 8 pm that day, but we did give him some water as it was a warm day and the puppy felt thirsty, he drank a little bit of it and did not touch it at all. At night when we gave him the food, which is Cerelac as recommended by the shopkeeper, the puppy ate only a little bit of it and the next day he did not eat anything, just had a little bit of water, and was continuously vomiting since day one. I called the shopkeeper, he suggested that I should feed him royal canin if he is not eating Cerelac, but I preferred consulting a vet before doing anything by myself, so I visited Sharanya Vets Clinic which is near my house and Dr. Shashank Tripathi was very generous and kind to me and he made me understand that I can be Parvovirus, as the symptoms were visible, he asked me to call the shopkeeper to ask if the dewarming process is completed or not, I called him around 4 to 5 times but the shopkeeper did not answer my calls neither did he call me back. The doctor prescribed Saline and injections for the puppy for 5 days and asked us to take proper care of the puppy. The next day there was blood visible in his diarrhea, which confirmed the Parvovirus disease. I called the shopkeeper to inform about this and he said that it is not his fault, etc etc and he started to fight with me, which made it clear that he was aware that the puppy is not fit but still he sold it to me. When I purchased the puppy, I brought him home and he went nowhere else, which makes it almost impossible to catch the virus after purchasing and the main cause of Parvovirus is improper dewarming and contact with infected dogs or contact with object infected by already infected dogs, which is not possible to happen at our home because we do not have any prior pets. The shopkeeper does not have a medical certificate, to certify that the puppy was fit at the time I purchased him and he has been arguing with me, on the fact that the puppy has caught the virus from the air, which is not possible. I want my full refund as it was his fault that he sold me a unfit puppy and did not provide me full information about the puppy health. As provided under Section 420 of Indian Penal Code and Section 318 of Bhartiya Nyaya Sanhita, cheating and dishonestly inducing the delivery of any property or love asset is a punishable offence. And I have the right to file a case against the shopkeeper under the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960, that the seller knowingly sold a sick puppy, which constitutes animal cruelty. We are trying the best from our side to save the little puppy from this disease and as soon as he recovers from this disease we will keep him in a pet home for proper care and concern, as I do not want to return the puppy to the shopkeeper because I am sure that he will not take care of him and not get him treated properly and kill him.
place_of_incident: Mahatma Sisir Kumar Sarani, Bag Bazar Colony, Ghosh Bagan, Baghbazar, Kolkata, West Bengal 700003

complaint:
I, Diksha Katyal, would like to inform you that I had employed Lalit Paswan for the past year to look after and care for my pet dogs, Pablo and Troy.

On the intervening night of 22nd and 23rd February 2025, at around 12:30 a.m., I woke up and noticed that Pablo was unable to walk properly and seemed traumatized, while Troy had visible bruises on his body and appeared scared. It seemed that both dogs had been badly beaten. When I questioned Lalit Paswan about this, he claimed ignorance of any such incident. However, I observed that his behavior was unusual, and he appeared to be intoxicated. I also noticed that he had an injury on his right hand. I informed my husband, and we decided to call a veterinarian in the morning.

On 23rd February 2025, we called Dr. D.J. Sen Choudhury, who arrived at our flat at around 9:30 a.m. After examining our dogs, he prescribed medication for Pablo, as his injuries were severe. He also advised an X-ray of Pablo,s left hind limb and left hip joint. Troy had minor injuries and did not require medication. Lalit Paswan was present during the examination, and his behavior remained abnormal.

Following the doctor,s advice, we took Pablo for an X-ray at Pet Care Diagnostic Center on the same day. Later that evening, Lalit Paswan suddenly informed me that he was not feeling well and would be leaving for his native place. Given the circumstances, my husband and I found his departure suspicious.

On the morning of 24th February 2025, around 11 a.m., we checked our building,s CCTV footage from 22nd February 2025. To our shock, the footage revealed that Lalit Paswan had taken both our dogs behind a parked car on the ground floor and started hitting them with a bamboo stick. Troy attempted to escape, but Lalit caught him, dragged him back, and took both dogs to the terrace. After about 10 minutes, he brought them back via the stairs, at which point Pablo was unable to walk properly. Then, on the second floor, Lalit tied Pablo to the staircase railing and brutally beat Troy, even banging him against the floor.

After seeing this shocking footage, I immediately called Lalit Paswan to confront him, but he completely denied the incident. Subsequently, he stopped reporting for duty and vacated the servant quarters with all his belongings.

On 25th February 2025, Dr. D.J. Sen Choudhury, after reviewing Pablo,s X-ray plates, confirmed that the injuries were consistent with being beaten with a stick.
In light of these events, I request you to kindly take a strong action against Lalit Paswan for the brutal assault on my pet dogs.
